calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-29-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For A Default Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-08-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Lynn O'Malley-Taylor, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840

George Holland, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Kenneth Andrews,
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.
Witness: Kenneth Andrews sworn and examined on behalf of Plaintiff.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant as follows:

Damages: $ 40,407.37
Pre-Judgment Interest: $ 0.00
Attorney's Fees: $0.00
Costs: $550.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $40,957.37

Judgment signed in open court.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-14-2016 Continued To Apr-19-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Set For Order To Show Cause On Feb-02-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2015 Continued To Nov-18-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Jul-27-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
George Holland, Sr., Attorney at Law, Tel. 510.465.4100, for plaintiffs;
Sierra S. Arballo, of Philip M. Andersen & Associates, Tel. 925.225.6838, for defendants, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT DAVID RICKEY'S Motion To Set Aside Default is denied. Plaintiff met his burden of establishing that personal service was properly effectuated. Evidence Code sec. 647 provides a presumption that the facts stated in the proof of service by a registered process server are true. (See American Express Centurion Bank v. Zara (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 383, holding that "[b]ecause of the statutory presumption, defendant was thus required to produce evidence that he was not served.") Here, Defendant has not produced sufficient evidence to overcome the presumption that he was personally served. The Declaration of Miles Pawski states that Defendant returns from Haiti to San Francisco "periodically" and that he has resided at the address where the purported personal service occurred. (Declaration of Miles Pawski, pars. 7-8.) Although Mr. Pawski states in a conclusory fashion that Defendant did not reside there on the date of service, Mr. Pawski provides no facts to support this contention. (Id. at par. 7.) The only specific fact provided about the address is that a tenant by the name of Mr. McCallum moved into the property in December 2014. (Id.) This fact does not establish that Defendant did not reside at the property on the date of service in August 2014. Order signed in open court. Judge Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/SRB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Jun-26-2015 Continued To Jul-27-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DAVID RICKEY'S Motion To Set Aside Default is continued to July 27, 2015, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, to give the opposing party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the opposition with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/SB)
